---
title: "Pigmentation gene network"
subtitle: "Knowing when a genetic variant causes a phenotype — by making independent lines of evidence converge"
---

## What this is

A model-system project on the genotype→phenotype map. **The goal is to know how much to trust a claimed link
between a genetic variant and a phenotype, by making independent lines of evidence converge** — statistical
association, mechanistic pathway knowledge, and experimental validation each capture part of the truth, and
where they agree we can be confident. Applied to human pigmentation, the aim is to grade reported pigmentation
associations by how well independent evidence converges on each, and to point to the specific next step that
would confirm it.

Pigmentation is the model system because it is highly heritable, has near-zero environmental variance, has a
well-characterized molecular mechanism, and shows the same prediction/portability failures — the same allele
causal in one population and silent in another — in a visible, checkable form.

## The work is in the notebooks

Each notebook is a **self-contained mini-manuscript** — introduction, methods, results, and
discussion/conclusion, with citations — that you can read, reproduce, and adapt. The processed tables are
their *outputs*; the notebooks are the contribution.

### [Notebook 1 — Reconstruct the published network](notebooks/01_reconstruct_published_network.ipynb)

Rebuilds the Raghunath et&nbsp;al. 2015 melanogenesis model as a **signed, directed** network — typing and
signing every interaction directly from the published file, with a resolvable citation for each edge. The
mechanistic backbone, and the most confident, foundational piece of work here.

### [Notebook 2 — Resolve the network to genes](notebooks/02_resolve_network_to_genes.ipynb)

Resolves the network's entities to genes (UniProt / HGNC / Ensembl / GRCh38), builds the explicit gene layer,
and validates every edge against OmniPath as a separate, non-destructive check. Every node and edge carries a
resolvable citation; a closing gate fails the build if anything is uncited.

### [Notebook 3 — Assemble the validation cases](notebooks/03_assemble_validation_cases.ipynb)

Documents how published cases of genotype→phenotype discordance were pulled from curated papers and counted,
paper by paper, with the numbers reproduced from the committed extracts. A reproducible, adaptable base for
testing the network.

## Status

This project is being reoriented toward the goal above; the precise contribution and a fuller, designed demo
are in progress. **Notebooks 1–2 are the stable core.** The current, authoritative account of the goal, the
materials, and the open decisions is in the repository — see `README.md` and `internal/START_HERE.md`.
